title: Error in Conditional Generate Construct
body:
The version of Verilator is Verilator 5.025 devel rev v5.024-5-g1012c054e.
The OS is a variant of Debian (version 12).


The following file

     cvw/src/uncore/uncore.sv

has the following generate blocks at line 142.

     if (P.UART_SUPPORTED == 1) begin : uart
          ...
     end else begin : uart
       assign UARTSout = 0; assign UARTIntr = 0;
     end

The above code causes Verilator to emit an error.  Changing the name of the second block from "uart" to "uart_bug" eliminates the error.

Verilator's handling of the above code is a bug because IEEE Std 1800-2023 allows "more than one block with the same name within a single conditional generate construct."

"uncore.sv" is part of the project at

     https://github.com/openhwgroup/cvw

on the branch with SHA1 ID dc6dc93b220e601d4829ffd466bdc0e000cfe0a0.

To produce this bug, replace the following files with the attached files

     cvw/src/generic/mem/rom1p1r.sv
     cvw/testbench/testbench.sv

and include the additional file "clangwrapper.c".  Then, run the following command.

verilator --trace --binary -j 0 -Wall -Wno-fatal -y ../config/shared -y ../config/rv32imc -top testbench ../src/cvw.sv ../src/hazard/*.sv ../src/mdu/*.sv ../src/wally/*.sv ../src/generic/*.sv ../src/privileged/*.sv ../src/ifu/bpred/*.sv ../src/ifu/*.sv ../src/cache/*.sv ../src/mmu/*.sv ../src/ebu/*.sv ../src/lsu/*.sv ../src/generic/flop/*.sv ../src/generic/mem/*.sv ./common/*.sv ../src/uncore/*.sv ../src/ieu/*.sv ./clangwrapper.c ./testbench.sv

I could not produce the bug by using a small sample of code.  The bug appears only during Verilating the full project.

[testbench_sv.txt](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/14960298/testbench_sv.txt)
[rom1p1r_sv.txt](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/14960301/rom1p1r_sv.txt)
[clangwrapper_c.txt](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/14960303/clangwrapper_c.txt)
